Title: Codon reassignment to facilitate genetic engineering and biocontainment in the chloroplast of Chlamydomonas reinhardtii

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Derived from chloroplast trnW locus in GenBank BK000554.2 positions 17481-17207; anticodon-mutated synthetic gene
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native trnW flanking promoter, endogenous chloroplast tRNA promoter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native chloroplast trnW locus; expression inferred from endogenous tRNA context
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in chloroplast transformants; sufficient to support UGA readthrough and rescue translation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mixotrophic and phototrophic growth at 25°C; 5-fluorocytosine treatment for CrCD assays; aerobic light selection for psaA rescue
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chloroplast transformation; Western blot rescue of CrCD; functional complementation of psaA W693TGA mutant; growth assays; PCR confirmation of homoplasmy
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables chloroplast translation of UGA as tryptophan, restoring full-length protein synthesis from TGA-containing transgenes and rescuing psaA function
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: crCD; psaA; tryptophanyl-tRNA synthetase (inferred charging partner)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No detectable growth defect under tested conditions; no effect on E. coli growth and no detectable UGA readthrough in E. coli
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key tool for chloroplast synthetic biology, allowing cloning/expression of genes toxic to E. coli and improving transgene biocontainment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Synthetic anticodon mutation CCA to UCA was introduced into the native chloroplast trnW sequence with 100-bp flanks. It likely inserts tryptophan at UGA codons and can serve as a positive selectable marker via psaA rescue.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Synthetic trnW_UCA enabled efficient UGA readthrough in the C. reinhardtii chloroplast. 2. It restored full-length, active CrCD from a TGA-mutated crCD transgene. 3. It complemented the essential psaA W693TGA mutation, indicating that the synthetic tRNA is charged with tryptophan. 4. It did not impair algal growth under tested conditions and was not functional for detectable readthrough in E. coli.

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Chloroplast psaA exon 3 locus; specific accession not provided
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Photosystem I reaction center core protein PsaA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Required for phototrophic growth under light and oxygen
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Aerobic light growth on minimal medium
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed chloroplast mutation of W693 TGG to TGA; phenotypic loss of phototrophy; rescue by trnW_UCA; PCR and sequencing
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation of the essential W693 codon to TGA abolished phototrophic growth, and trnW_UCA restored photosynthesis-compatible PsaA function
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Serves as an in vivo reporter/selection target demonstrating that UGA can be reassigned specifically to tryptophan in the chloroplast
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: W693 in PsaA is important for interaction with bound phylloquinone; rescue of the TGA mutant supported that trnW_UCA inserts tryptophan rather than another amino acid.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Changing psaA W693 from TGG to TGA caused a PSI-deficient, light-sensitive, non-phototrophic phenotype. 2. Introduction of trnW_UCA rescued this defect without reversion of the psaA codon. 3. The rescue demonstrates accurate tryptophan insertion at UGA in chloroplast translation.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: psaA exon 1 promoter, constitutive chloroplast promoter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in chloroplast transformants carrying pSty
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Codon optimization with one TGG to TGA change; cloning into pWUCA2; chloroplast transformation; PCR and Western blot
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The toxic endolysin gene could be cloned and expressed in C. reinhardtii chloroplast using the trnW_UCA system
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product is toxic to E. coli if expressed
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates practical utility of codon reassignment for handling bacteriotoxic proteins in chloroplast engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a proof-of-utility toxic gene rather than a nutrient-metabolism gene.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A codon-modified SPN9CC endolysin gene containing an internal TGA codon was clonable via the trnW_UCA system. 2. The protein accumulated in chloroplast transformants. 3. The experiment demonstrated that genes toxic to E. coli can be engineered using this codon reassignment strategy.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: psaA exon 1 promoter, constitutive chloroplast promoter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in chloroplast transformants carrying pSde
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Codon optimization with two TGG to TGA changes; cloning into pWUCA2; chloroplast transformation; PCR and Western blot
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The highly E. coli-toxic Sden_1266 gene could be cloned and expressed in C. reinhardtii chloroplast using the trnW_UCA system
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product is very toxic to E. coli upon expression
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Further validates the codon reassignment platform for expressing genes that cannot be readily handled in E. coli cloning hosts
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a proof-of-utility toxic gene rather than a nutrient-metabolism gene.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A codon-modified Sden_1266 gene containing two internal TGA codons was successfully cloned using the trnW_UCA platform. 2. The protein accumulated in C. reinhardtii chloroplast transformants. 3. This supports the general utility of the system for otherwise unclonable, bacteriotoxic genes.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: psaA exon 1 promoter, constitutive chloroplast promoter
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates cytosine to uracil and converts 5-fluorocytosine to toxic 5-fluorouracil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cytotoxic compound causing cell death; used as negative selection product after 5-fluorocytosine conversion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in chloroplast transformants under psaA exon 1 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-fluorocytosine treatment; mixotrophic or phototrophic growth at 25°C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Codon replacement (TGG to TGA); chloroplast transformation; Western blot; 5-fluorocytosine sensitivity assay; rescue by co-introduced trnW_UCA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional CrCD accumulates only when trnW_UCA is present, restoring 5-fluorocytosine sensitivity via 5-fluorouracil production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful negative selectable marker and proof-of-concept enzyme for chloroplast codon reassignment; supports engineering of metabolic enzymes toxic to E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A chloroplast codon-optimized E. coli gene used as test transgene; two internal Trp codons were mutated to TGA (W21 and W147). It is not naturally toxic to E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Introducing two TGA codons into crCD abolished detectable full-length CrCD accumulation in the C. reinhardtii chloroplast. 2. Co-expression of synthetic trnW_UCA restored full-length CrCD accumulation and enzymatic activity. 3. Restored CrCD converted 5-fluorocytosine into toxic 5-fluorouracil, causing growth inhibition.
